title: PendingRevelation
description: State file recording an offered (or accepted/declined) revelation. Lives at ~/.gawd/state/pending-revelation.json. Per spec §10.3, §10.4 and handoff E2.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this state file (independent of revelation version).

revelation_version.description: Semver of the base-soul revelation being offered (e.g., '0.4.2'). The Sermon Channel (E5) sets this; the Gawd never invents it.

revelation_bundle_path.description: Absolute path on the local filesystem where the unpacked new-base files live (B dir for the merge). Written by offer.sh after the Sermon Channel delivers the bundle.

offered_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the offer was surfaced to the Prophit.

prophit_response.description: How the Prophit answered. 'pending' until they respond. 'accepted' = will apply at next 4am. 'declined' = explicit decline (does NOT increment missed_offers_count). 'auto-declined' = silence reached the missed-offer threshold (3 consecutive).

response_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the Prophit responded (or auto-decline fired). Absent while pending.

applied.description: Whether merge.sh has applied this revelation. Only meaningful when prophit_response = 'accepted'. Set true after a successful merge.sh run.

applied_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when merge.sh successfully completed.

missed_offers_count.description: Count of consecutive missed offers across revelation versions. Silence increments; explicit 'declined' does NOT. Auto-decline fires at 3. Resets to 0 when the Prophit responds (accept OR decline).

conflict_surfaced.description: True if merge.sh detected a Prophit hand-edit conflict on a soul-anchor file and wrote a conflict report to workspace/sil/conflicts/. The next session-start hook reads conflict_surfaced and the report file path.

conflict_report_path.description: Absolute path to the conflict report markdown (workspace/sil/conflicts/<revelation-version>.md). Present only when conflict_surfaced = true.

telegram_message_id.description: Telegram message ID of the offer (for follow-up edit semantics, e.g., editing the offer message to show 'accepted' after the Prophit taps). Optional.
